xposts.title: Hard problem vanishes when world & consciousness are brain simulations
xposts.excerpt: The hard problem goes away once you accept that the world you experience is a simulation (created by your brain), and that this includes the experience of consciousness itself. It's not that minds are at a different plane of existence than objects, colors, people, but physics is.

xposts.title: Consciousness is virtual; only simulations can have phenomenal experience
xposts.excerpt: Koch thinks that a simulation cannot be conscious, but he got it exactly backwards. Physical objects cannot be conscious, because consciousness is virtual: phenomenal experience can only exist in a simulation.

xposts.title: Experiencing self is a simulation solving the hard problem
xposts.excerpt: Personally, I think that the functional role of consciousness is approximately captured by the intersection of the ideas of Graziano, Bengio, Dennett, Egan, Drescher and others (~control model of attention), and the hard problem by the experiencing self being a simulation.

xposts.title: Human consciousness itself is a simulation recreatable in AI
xposts.excerpt: I think that human consciousness is itself a simulation, and one that can eventually be recreated in important aspects by AI models. If we give the AI system an identity born out of a continuous experience of the interaction between world and motivated self, its art becomes real.

xposts.title: Self and external universe are both representations in the same mind
xposts.excerpt: The separation between first person self and external universe is an illusion that breaks down during non dual experiences. That’s not because you are somehow partaking in external cosmic consciousness, but because self and external universe are representations in the same mind.

xposts.title: Conscious self is part of the brain's virtual simulation of world
xposts.excerpt: Would you say that the worlds of Minecraft or Quake are physical? I think they are virtual worlds. The world you experience is the result of a game engine running on your brain, tracking sensory data. Your own conscious self is part of the simulation. Self and world are virtual.

xposts.title: Consciousness is the representation of what it would be like to exist
xposts.excerpt: From the perspective of the implementation, consciousness is not what it is like, but the representation of what it would be like. We are not on the side of the things that exist but on the side of what it would be like to exist.

xposts.title: Every object including self exists only as a mental construction
xposts.excerpt: One of the most important insights: every object you observe, conceptualize or experience is a construction in your mind, created to explain sensory patterns. The universe never gives you more than patterns. God, self, photons exist in your mind. Reality is unknowable.

terms.title: Virtual Self
terms.definition: The experiencing self is a simulation created by the brain as part of its overall model of reality. Self and world are both virtual constructs within the same mental simulation; phenomenal experience arises only inside this simulation.

terms.title: Phenomenal Simulation
terms.definition: Consciousness and all experienced phenomena are virtual representations rather than direct access to physical reality. The brain runs a simulation that includes the experience of consciousness itself, dissolving the hard problem.
